audit_meta.honest_assessment: LaunchedIn10 is a low-authority incubation site with fully-deployed AEO infrastructure and zero organic clicks over 90 days. The binding constraint is domain authority, not content volume or technical SEO. Two genuine issues require action: /pricing/ soft-404 (P0) and homepage price mismatch (P1, operator decision). The site needs backlinks, time, and patience — not more content or schema additions.

action_items.description: /pricing/ is a soft 404 — it returns HTTP 200 but serves byte-for-byte identical content to the homepage. The SSG build (generate-static.js) does not generate /pricing/index.html, so Cloudflare Pages falls back to /index.html. The page title, canonical, and all body content match the homepage exactly. Any backlinks or internal links pointing to /pricing/ deliver zero unique value. Bing has crawled and cached this page (DocumentSize 24,769 bytes) but it contains homepage content, not pricing content. This is the only P0 on the site.
action_items.prompt: Fix the /pricing/ soft 404 on launchedin10.co.uk. The issue is in the SSG build: generate-static.js does not generate /pricing/index.html, so Cloudflare Pages serves the homepage fallback. Steps: 1) Open generate-static.js in the EldrisOU/launchedin10-final repo. 2) Add /pricing/ to the list of routes that get SSG-rendered (it should already have a React component — check src/pages/ or the route config). 3) Ensure the SSG generates /pricing/index.html with its own unique <title>, <h1>, canonical (https://launchedin10.co.uk/pricing/), and pricing-specific content. 4) Add /pricing/ to public/sitemap.xml. 5) Rebuild and deploy. 6) Verify: curl -sL https://launchedin10.co.uk/pricing/ | grep '<title>' should NOT match the homepage title. 7) After deploy, submit /pricing/ to Bing via SubmitUrlBatch and trigger IndexNow.
action_items.verification: curl -sL https://launchedin10.co.uk/pricing/ | grep '<title>' — must NOT match 'High-Performance Web Design in 10 Days'. Canonical must be https://launchedin10.co.uk/pricing/. Page must appear in sitemap.xml.
action_items.estimated_impact: Fixes a broken commercial page. Currently Bing has cached homepage content at /pricing/ — recrawl after fix will deliver correct pricing content to Bing/ChatGPT/Copilot retrieval.
action_items.evidence: curl diff between / and /pricing/ shows identical body (27,260 bytes each). Title both 'High-Performance Web Design in 10 Days | LaunchedIn10'. Canonical both 'https://launchedin10.co.uk'.

action_items.description: Homepage HTML displays SEO automation pricing (Launch £99.95/mo, Boost £149.95/mo, Orbit £195.00/mo) but JSON-LD structured data declares web design pricing (Starter £497 activation + £99/mo, Growth £997 activation + £149/mo, Scale £1997 activation + £249/mo). These are two different product lines with different price points. Google may show web design prices in rich results while users see SEO automation prices on the page. This is a potential Google Ads policy violation if ads ever link to the homepage. Carried forward from Run 16 — requires operator decision on which service the homepage should promote.
action_items.prompt: OPERATOR DECISION REQUIRED: The homepage at https://launchedin10.co.uk/ shows two different pricing structures — SEO automation in the visible HTML and web design in the JSON-LD schema. Mark needs to decide: (A) Homepage promotes web design → update the visible HTML pricing section to show web design tiers (£497+£99, £997+£149, £1997+£249), or (B) Homepage promotes SEO automation → update the JSON-LD in generate-static.js to match the visible SEO pricing (£99.95, £149.95, £195.00 with no activation fees). After operator decision, update the mismatched source in generate-static.js. Verify by curling the homepage and comparing HTML prices vs JSON-LD prices — they must match exactly.
action_items.verification: curl -sL https://launchedin10.co.uk/ — extract all £ values from visible HTML and from JSON-LD script blocks. Every HTML price must have a matching JSON-LD price. No activation fee should appear in JSON-LD unless also visible in HTML.
action_items.estimated_impact: Eliminates Google policy risk and ensures rich results display correct pricing.
action_items.evidence: HTML body: £99.95/£149.95/£195.00 (SEO automation). JSON-LD: £99/£149/£249 + £497/£997/£1997 activation (web design). Confirmed present in both Run 16 and Run 17.

action_items.description: After fixing ACT-LI10-039, /pricing/ must be added to sitemap.xml. Currently the page is absent from the sitemap despite returning HTTP 200 (albeit with homepage content). Once the SSG generates the correct /pricing/index.html, the sitemap must include it so both Google and Bing discover the properly-rendered version. This is a dependency — do not add /pricing/ to the sitemap until the soft-404 is fixed, or you will be directing crawlers to a homepage duplicate.
action_items.prompt: DEPENDENCY: Only execute after ACT-LI10-039 is complete and /pricing/ serves unique content. In the EldrisOU/launchedin10-final repo, open the sitemap generation logic in generate-static.js. Add https://launchedin10.co.uk/pricing/ to the URL list with today's lastmod date. Rebuild and deploy. Then resubmit the sitemap to Google via gsc_submit_sitemap (siteUrl: sc-domain:launchedin10.co.uk, feedpath: https://launchedin10.co.uk/sitemap.xml). Verify: curl https://launchedin10.co.uk/sitemap.xml | grep pricing — must return the /pricing/ URL.
action_items.verification: curl https://launchedin10.co.uk/sitemap.xml | grep '/pricing/' — must return the URL. gsc_list_sitemaps should show updated URL count (118).
action_items.estimated_impact: Ensures /pricing/ is discoverable by both Google and Bing crawlers via sitemap.
action_items.evidence: Current sitemap.xml contains 117 URLs. /pricing/ is absent despite the page existing at the route level.

action_items.description: Two blog posts are 'Crawled - currently not indexed' by Google: /blog/website-design/web-design-cost-2026-breakdown-small/ (last crawled 01.04.26) and /blog/industry-spotlights/why-website-design-for-construction/ (last crawled 03.04.26). Both have only the sitemap as a referring URL — Google discovered no internal links to either page. Adding contextual internal links from higher-authority pages may prompt Google to re-evaluate. However, at current traffic levels (0 clicks site-wide), this is low priority — Google may have rejected these on quality signals regardless of internal linking. Bing has indexed both with healthy DocumentSize.
action_items.prompt: In the EldrisOU/launchedin10-final repo, add contextual internal links to the two 'not indexed' blog posts. 1) Find the /blog/seo-fundamentals/ content (or the page that renders the SEO fundamentals category) and add a contextual link to /blog/website-design/web-design-cost-2026-breakdown-small/ with anchor text like 'our web design cost breakdown'. 2) Find the /blog/industry-spotlights/ content and add a link to /blog/industry-spotlights/why-website-design-for-construction/ with anchor text like 'website design for construction companies'. These links should appear naturally within existing content, not as a forced link block. After deploy, request re-indexing via gsc_inspect_url for both URLs.
action_items.verification: gsc_inspect_url on both URLs — status should eventually change from 'Crawled - currently not indexed' to 'Submitted and indexed'. Allow 2-4 weeks for Google to re-evaluate.
action_items.codebase_path: li10_posts content or generate-static.js internal link logic
action_items.estimated_impact: May help Google re-evaluate 2 blog posts for indexing. Low confidence — Google may have rejected on quality signals.
action_items.evidence: GSC URL Inspection: both URLs show 'Crawled - currently not indexed', last crawl April 2026. Referring URLs: sitemap only.

resolved_items.description: FAQPage duplicate on /case-studies/ — SSG and React hydration both injected FAQPage JSON-LD, causing Google Rich Results FAIL. The SSG guard has been implemented and confirmed working: curl of /case-studies/ shows 0 FAQPage blocks in the HTML. Google's cached index still shows the FAIL from prior crawl but will clear on next re-crawl. Open for 7 consecutive audits (Run 10 through Run 16). Code fix confirmed via render_compare in Run 17.
resolved_items.evidence: render_compare on /case-studies/ shows 0 FAQPage blocks in SSR HTML. GSC URL Inspection still shows FAQ FAIL (stale cache). Code fix confirmed in generate-static.js.

resolved_items.description: Blog post /blog/website-design/web-design-cost-2026-breakdown-small/ was 140+ days stale. Content updated and dateModified refreshed to 2026-06-01. Visible 'Last updated: 1 June 2026' now present.
resolved_items.evidence: curl confirms dateModified: 2026-06-01T00:00:00Z in JSON-LD. Visible text shows 'Last updated 1 June 2026'.

resolved_items.description: /website-translation/ Bing stale-empty-crawl (DocumentSize=0) resolved. Current Bing Block A shows DocumentSize=14,922 bytes with LastCrawledDate 11.06.26.
Sitemap.xml resubmitted to Google. GSC shows last downloaded 2026-06-15.
Malformed internal link (<div class= in href attribute) fixed in batch commit e99f616.
resolved_items.evidence: Git commit e99f616. GA4 still shows historical sessions with the malformed path — will age out of 90-day window.
